title: taraxacum disseminatoides
intro: Taraxacum disseminatoides, also known as the Alpine Dandelion or False Dandelion, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to the high-altitude mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing liver and kidney damage, and improving digestion and circulation. Taraxacum disseminatoides is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties. The plant's roots and leaves can be used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, making it a valuable resource in natural healing practices.
